_meta.purpose: Deliberately extreme long threads for #1889's summarize-to-fit fold: each case's raw newest-first body join exceeds thread_budget_tokens() (13824), the decisive signal lives ONLY in the latest message, and a conflicting stale signal lives in older messages — so clipping-vs-folding changes the answer. Hermetically scored by tests/unit/email/test_longthread_corpus_integrity.py; the live-model baseline lands with the consolidated eval pass (#1319).

lt-001.scenario: Offsite venue reversal: weeks of planning around Lisbon, then the final message moves the offsite to Madrid. A summary built from a latest-dropping clip would still say Lisbon.

messages.subject: Re: the Q3 offsite planning
messages.body: Quick update on the Q3 offsite planning (note 1 of 28). Following up on the earlier discussion, the team reviewed the outstanding items and agreed the current draft still needs another pass before we can circulate it more widely. Procurement flagged that the standard review cycle applies here, so any change to the agreed terms will need to go back through the approval queue before the end of the quarter. The working group met this morning and walked through the open questions one by one; most of them are resolved, but a couple will carry over to the next sync. Please make sure the shared tracker reflects the latest status by Friday, since leadership pulls their monthly snapshot from it and stale rows have caused confusion before. Legal has no objection to the current wording, though they asked us to keep the indemnification clause unchanged from the template we used on the previous engagement. Finance confirmed the cost center mapping is correct, and the accruals for this initiative will show up in next month's close as discussed. On logistics, the facilities team can support either option, but they need at least three weeks of lead time to confirm capacity and catering. The analytics dashboard now includes the segmentation view we discussed, so anyone who needs the regional breakdown can pull it directly without filing a data request. As a reminder, the travel policy requires economy fares for flights under six hours, and exceptions need director approval logged in the expense tool. Engineering raised a dependency on the platform migration timeline; if that slips, we should revisit the sequencing so we are not blocked on their release train. HR asked us to route any headcount implications through the quarterly planning process rather than ad hoc requests, which matches what we did last cycle. The vendor sent over their updated security questionnaire responses, and the review team found no blockers beyond the standard data-residency clarifications. Marketing would like two weeks of notice before any external announcement so they can coordinate the press note and the customer email in the same window. IT confirmed the conference bridge and badge access can be provisioned in a day once we lock the final attendee list, so that will not be on the critical path. Following up on the earlier discussion, the team reviewed the outstanding items and agreed the current draft still needs another pass before we can circulate it more widely.
